name: QRadar-0005 - Large-Scale Data Exfiltration Detection
description: Detects potential data exfiltration by monitoring for unusually large outbound data transfers from internal hosts to external IP addresses. Triggers when a single host transmits more than 500 MB to an external destination within a 30-minute window, which may indicate data theft via FTP, HTTP/S, or custom protocols.

detection.aql: SELECT sourceIP, destinationIP, destinationPort, SUM(bytesOut) as total_bytes_out, COUNT(*) as flow_count FROM flows WHERE IsInternal(sourceIP) = TRUE AND IsInternal(destinationIP) = FALSE AND bytesOut IS NOT NULL AND destinationPort NOT IN (80, 443, 53) GROUP BY sourceIP, destinationIP, destinationPort HAVING SUM(bytesOut) >= 524288000 LAST 30 MINUTES

response.offense_name: Data Exfiltration Detected from {sourceIP} to {destinationIP} — {total_bytes_out} bytes
response.offense_description: An internal host has transferred more than 500 MB of data to an external IP address within 30 minutes. This may indicate unauthorized data exfiltration or a compromised insider.

notes: Inspired by SIGMA rule: net_data_exfiltration_over_non_http. Adjust threshold based on normal business traffic baselines. Whitelist known backup destinations, CDN endpoints, and cloud sync services. Correlate with DLP alerts for higher confidence.
author: Security Team
